data.title: AgriFORCE to conduct reverse stock split
data.description:
• AgriFORCE Growing Systems (NASDAQ:AGRI) said it will conduct a reverse stock split.
  • In the split every one 100 shares will automatically be combined into one share.
  • The split is being implemented for the purpose of regaining compliance with the minimum bid price requirement.

data.title: Live Nation Entertainment dips on plans to offer $1B convertible debt
data.description:
Live Nation Entertainment (NYSE:LYV) on Tuesday announced plans to offer $1B in aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes due 2030.

The notes will accrue interest payable semi-annually in arrears and will mature on Jan. 15, 2030, unless repurchased, redeemed or converted in accordance with their terms prior to such date.

The company expects to grant the initial purchasers a 13-day option to purchase up to an additional $100M of the notes.

It expects to use the net proceeds from the offering to fund the repurchase of a portion of its 2.00% convertible senior notes due 2025; to repay outstanding amounts under the company's existing revolving credit facility; to pay related fees and expenses; and for general corporate purposes.

Shares of the entertainment company were down over 5% premarket.

data.title: JP Morgan staying 'sidelined' on Super Micro even as committee finds no wrongdoing
data.description:
• Even as an independent special committee has found no wrongdoing, investment firm J.P. Morgan said it is staying "sidelined" on Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) until it gets better visibility into compliance issues.
  • Shares rose 5% in premarket trading.
  • "In our view, the next key watch points for investors to monitor include: whether the new independent auditors, BDO, accept the findings of the Special Committee or decide to undertake their own independent review; and whether Nasdaq supports Super Micro’s request for an extension of time to regain compliance with the Nasdaq continued listing requirements, where the company has already outlined its intention to complete its 10-K and 10-Q filings and become current within the discretionary period available," analyst Samik Chatterjee wrote in a note to clients. Chatterjee kept his Underweight rating on Super Micro.
  • Super Micro said on Monday that an independent special committee found no evidence of misconduct by the board of directors or audit committee. The company also said it does not expect to restate its financial statements based on the committee's review, thought it did recommend hiring a new CFO.

data.title: AT&T expects profit growth to accelerate through 2027, sets $20B buyback
data.description:
AT&T (NYSE:T) expects a double-digit percentage growth in adjusted EPS and free cash flow of more than $18B in 2027, the telecoms giant said on Tuesday as it embarks on a multi-year strategic plan highlighted by continued profitable 5G and fiber subscriber growth.

The wireless carrier unveiled a strategy to expand its fiber broadband network to 50 million+ total locations by the end of 2029 and complete the modernization of its 5G wireless network with open technology by 2027.

It further expects improved financial performance to support $40 billion+ of anticipated shareholder returns through dividends and share repurchases over the next three years, including an initial share repurchase authorization of $10B that management expects to complete by the end of 2026.

Beginning in the first quarter of 2025, and as a result of the pending disposition of our DIRECTV equity method investment, AT&T (T) plans to report adjusted EPS and free cash flow excluding earnings and cash flows related to DIRECTV.

It projects adjusted EPS, excluding DIRECTV, of $1.97 to $2.07 (consensus estimate: $2.27) in 2025, accelerating to double-digit percentage growth in 2027. Free cash flow, excluding DIRECTV, is estimated at more than $16B in 2025, with annual growth of around $1B, resulting in free cash flow of $18 billion+ in 2027.

Consolidated service revenue growth is seen in the low-single-digit range annually from 2025-2027.

For this year, the firm expects adjusted EPS of $2.20 to $2.25 (consensus estimate: $2.21), with around 3% growth in wireless service revenue growth.

The company expects this plan to provide $50B+ of financial capacity over the next three years, largely through organic growth, and return $40B+ of this financial capacity. The plan would result in $20B+ in total dividend payments, with capacity for about $20B in share repurchases, from 2025-2027.

AT&T (T) expects to maintain its current annualized common stock dividend of $1.11 a share.

Shares of the wireless carrier fell 3.6% premarket initially, but were up around 2% at the time of publication.

data.title: Spire Global names COO Condor as new CEO; Platzer to become Executive Chairman
data.description:
Spire Global (NYSE:SPIR) said Tuesday it has appointed current COO Theresa Condor as its new CEO effective January 1, while founder and CEO Peter Platzer will become Executive Chairman as part of a planned leadership transition.

Condor was part of Spire's (SPIR) founding team, and as COO, she directly oversees the company's Space Services, Weather, and Aviation business units worldwide, including U.S. Federal and Global Government; previously she worked in emerging markets finance.

Platzer founded Spire Global (SPIR) and has served as CEO since the company's inception.

The company also appointed two former Airbus executives to two key roles, Celia Pelaz as COO, and Gabriel Oehme as Chief Transformation Officer.

data.title: Coherus BioSciences to divest UDENYCA franchise for up to $558.4M
data.description:
Coherus BioSciences (NASDAQ:CHRS) said it has entered into an asset purchase agreement with Intas Pharmaceuticals for the divestiture of the UDENYCA franchise for up to $558.4 million.

Shares of the firm rose 27.7% in premarket trade.

The deal price includes an upfront payment of $483.4 million, and $75 million in potential net sales milestone payments.

The firm expects to use a portion of the dea proceeds to fully repay the entirety of its $230 million in existing convertible notes due April 2026 and $49.1 million to buy-out certain royalty obligations related to UDENYCA.

Intas will receive identified assets related to the UDENYCA franchise.

At close, the firm will pay $49.1 million to buy out the right to receive royalties on net sales of UDENYCA.

The company also expects to realize substantial cost savings on a going forward basis by paying off certain financial liabilities, transferring certain full-time employees to Intas to support UDENYCA, and eliminating UDENYCA-related overhead and commercial expenses.

At current, the company said the post-close cash runway projections exceed two years, past key data readouts expected in 2026.

“The proposed divestiture of UDENYCA represents the successful execution of our strategy to focus R&D and commercial resources on Coherus’ innovative immuno-oncology portfolio and to strengthen our financial position,” said Denny Lanfear, Coherus CEO and chair.

data.title: Enbridge declares raises dividend by 3% to CAD 0.9425
data.description:
• Enbridge (NYSE:ENB) declares CAD 0.9425/share quarterly dividend, 3% increase from prior dividend of CAD 0.9150.
  • Forward yield 6.19%
  • Payable March 1; for shareholders of record Feb. 14; ex-div Feb. 14.
  • See ENB Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Matador Resources expands credit deal
data.description:
• Matador Resources Company (NYSE:MTDR) has announced a $750M increase in borrowing base under its credit agreement to $3.25B.
  • This increase was unanimously supported by each of the 19 lenders under Matador’s credit facility. It resulted from the regularly scheduled semi-annual borrowing base redetermination, although Matador elected to keep the borrowing commitments at $2.25B at present but appreciates the potential to increase its borrowings to $3.25B at a later date.
  • As of Nov. 30, 2024, Matador had $830M outstanding under its revolving credit facility with approximately $1.4B in liquidity.
  • In addition, the company’s midstream joint venture, San Mateo Midstream, amended and restated its credit agreement to increase the lender commitments by ~50% from $535M to $800M and extend the maturity date to Nov. 2029.

data.title: China halts exports of gallium, germanium to U.S. in response to chip sanctions
data.description:
China has banned exports of "dual-use items" related to gallium, germanium, antimony and super hard materials to the U.S. with immediate effect, its commerce ministry said on Tuesday.

Beijing's directive on items with both military and civilian use, cites national security concerns, and comes a day after Washington's latest crackdown on China's chip sector.

"In principle, the export of gallium, germanium, antimony, and superhard materials to the United States shall not be permitted," the ministry said.

Gallium and germanium are used in semiconductors, while germanium is also used in infrared technology, fibre optic cables and solar cells.

China supplies 60% of the world’s germanium, prices of which hit record highs earlier this year in China.

President Xi Jinping’s government had last year placed the rare metals under stricter government oversight as tensions flared between the world’s largest economies.

President-elect Donald Trump has threatened new tariffs on Mexico, Canada and China - the top three trading partners of the U.S. - soon after he is sworn in. During the campaign trail, Trump had pledged to increase tariffs on Chinese imports by 60%-100%.

data.title: Dow names Karen Carter as COO
data.description:
• Dow (NYSE:DOW) said it has appointed Karen Carter as the COO, effective immediately.
  • Carter, who joined the firm in 1994, is currently president of Dow Packaging & Specialty Plastics.
  • She has held a broad progression of leadership positions in her more than 30-year career with Dow, including business operations, sales and marketing, and human resources.
  • Carter will also join the company's executive committee, and will continue to be based in Midland, Michigan.
